name: Peg Leg Bates
abstract: Clayton "Peg Leg" Bates (October 11, 1907 – December 6, 1998) was an African-American entertainer from Fountain Inn, South Carolina, United States.

concerts.label: Jazz Concert featuring Duke Ellington and Others
Jazz Concert featuring Duke Ellington and Others
Nat King Cole, Sarah Vaughan, Ralph Marterie, and Illinois Jacquet
Midnight Performance: Nat King Cole, Sarah Vaughan, Ralph Marterie, and Illinois Jacquet

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmy Jones
about_quotes.evidence: we had -- Duke was the emcee, and we had Patterson and Jackson, comedians, Timmy Rogers, and then Stump and Stumpy, some more comedians, you know, and a dancer named Peg Leg Bates.

about_quotes.speaker: Red Holloway
about_quotes.evidence: But like you had tap dancers, you had comedians, you had novelty acts, Butter Beans and Suzie, and Peg Leg Bates, you had a real collection of people to make up a show.
about_quotes.doc_title: Red Holloway interviewed by Monk Rowe and Michael Woods, Scottsdale, Arizona, March 4, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: Leonidas Raymond Young
about_quotes.evidence: And on that show it was Perry Como and Nat Cole, Roy Hamilton, Ralph Marterie band, Peg Leg Bates, Sarah Vaughn, and a cast of thousands, I guess you could say.

about_quotes.speaker: Louie Bellson
about_quotes.evidence: Now besides that they had Peg Leg Bates, Timmy Rodgers, Stump and Stumpy, Patterson and Jackson, all these wonderful acts — tap dancing acts, you know.
about_quotes.doc_title: Louie Bellson interviewed by Monk Rowe, Sarasota, Florida, April 12, 1996

about_quotes.speaker: Nellie Lutcher
about_quotes.evidence: Peg Leg Bates, I remember him. I think Peg Leg is still around. And I can remember him performing and dancing with this peg leg, you know.

about_quotes.speaker: Patricia Willard
about_quotes.evidence: on the show I don't have to tell you it was Peg Leg Bates, Timmie Rodgers, Patteson and Jackson, just all heavy weights in show business.
